codeReview.summary: PanelHeader was extracted cleanly, but BrandMark was only partially extracted: the new component renders just the ghost icon while the wordmark and tagline remain inline in EditorView.

issues.description: `BrandMark` only renders the ghost SVG. The feature description requires the standalone component to render the full brand mark (ghost icon + wordmark + tagline), but `EditorView` still keeps the text cluster inline at `src/editor/EditorView.tsx:69-73`. The branding cluster therefore was not fully extracted into `src/ui/BrandMark.tsx`, and consumers of `@/ui` cannot reuse the complete brand mark.

The new BrandMark tests only verify the SVG/icon, aria-hidden, and token usage. They never assert the required wordmark/tagline content or that the full brand cluster moved out of `EditorView`, which is why the incomplete extraction above passed unnoticed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-redesign` skill says workers must invoke `frontend-design` at the start of the session, but this worker only invoked `frontend-redesign`. The handoff still marks `followedProcedure` as true, so the skill instructions and review expectations are currently out of sync.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `D:\coding\somewhat\.factory\skills\frontend-redesign\SKILL.md:21` says to invoke `frontend-design` at the START. The transcript skeleton for worker session `0b16c573-ffd2-4cfa-8a3c-9f913babcdc1` shows `Skill: mission-worker-base` and later `Skill: frontend-redesign`, with no `frontend-design` call, while the handoff JSON records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail. PanelHeader extraction looks correct, but BrandMark is incomplete: only the ghost icon moved to `src/ui`, while the wordmark/tagline remain inline in `EditorView`.
